channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 1 and injuring others
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion and fire rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague on Saturday, killing one woman and injuring other people and destroying several apartments, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster is unclear.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Storm Darragh batters UK and Ireland, leaving 1 dead and hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A man died and hundreds of thousands of people in Britain and Ireland were left without power Saturday as high winds and heavy rain battered the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Austin Tice: Family of journalist kidnapped in Syria 12 years ago say he's still alive, citing ‘vetted' source
item.description: The family of Austin Tice, a former Marine turned journalist who was kidnapped by jihadist militants in 2012 while reporting in Syria, say he is still alive, citing a “significant source.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: Nearly four days after the shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, police still do not know the gunman’s name or whereabouts or have a motive for the killing.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' to be auctioned nearly 20 years after theft
item.description: Nearly two decades after a pair of ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” were stolen from a Minnesota museum, the iconic shoes are set to be auctioned off to the highest bidder Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson remained elusive Friday, even as investigators learned more about his movements before and after the shocking crime. Investigators still don't know his name. They haven't found the gun.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court upholds law ordering China-based ByteDance to sell TikTok or face U.S. ban
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KTLA 5

item.title: Data reveals the 'most popular' Christmas song in Nevada
item.description: Which Christmas songs do we just love to love, at least until we get sick of Christmas music and forget about it until late into the next calendar year? Google just might have the answer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: A federal judge has upheld the consideration of race in admissions at the U.S. Naval Academy, ruling that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the officer training school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial urged jurors on Friday to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn't reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge upholds Naval Academy's race-conscious admissions policy
item.description: A federal judge upheld the Naval Academy’s race-conscious admissions policy Friday, rejecting a challenge from the group that succeeded at the Supreme Court in gutting affirmative action at civilian higher education institutions.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting used fake ID at hostel: sources
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown is believed to have used a fake New Jersey ID to check into an Upper West Side hostel before the shooting, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: 186 minors cited in Utah bar sting
item.description: Over 180 citations were issued to minors for being inside a Sugar House bar following an investigation into concerns of "serious violations" of Utah's alcohol laws.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Father of victim in NYC subway chokehold case sues defendant Daniel Penny
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The father of the victim at the center of the fatal New York City subway chokehold trial has sued the defendant as a Manhattan jury continued to deliberate the case Thursday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts hit 6 months in space. Just 2 more to go
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Known across the globe as the stuck astronauts, Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams hit the six-month mark in space Thursday with two more to go.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Secret Service director touts changes as Congress presses him on Trump assassination attempt
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting director of the Secret Service said Thursday that the agency is “reorganizing and reimagining” its culture and how it operates following an assassination attempt against Donald Trump on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: US judge rejects Boeing's plea deal in a conspiracy case stemming from fatal plane crashes
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday rejected a deal that would have let Boeing plead guilty to a felony conspiracy charge and pay a fine for misleading U.S. regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASA pushes back astronaut flights to the moon again
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA announced more delays Thursday in sending astronauts back to the moon more than 50 years after Apollo. Administrator Bill Nelson said the next mission in the Artemis program -- flying four astronauts around the moon and back – is now targeted for April 2026.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to cap anesthesia coverage after a set time limit
item.description: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield said in a statement that its decision to backpedal resulted from “significant widespread misinformation” about the policy.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Higgins, ABC 4

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, ABC 4

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, KRON4

item.title: Illinois homeowner feels underground mine is splitting her house in two
item.description: About 330,000 Illinois homes sit on top of old abandoned mines. Some say their homes are sinking because of it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Ammunition from UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting had words written on it: sources
item.description: Bullets found on the ground where UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot Wednesday had writing on them, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Kala Rama, WGN 9

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The right frame can freeze a moment in time, creating meaning for the masses from a fist pump over a bloodied ear, a bridge crumbled by a ship, towns shredded by nature, and a victory sealed with an on-field kiss. In 2024, photographers across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest publishing launch of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump moves to toss Georgia criminal case as president-elect
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday asked to throw out his criminal election interference charges in Georgia following his election victory. Trump’s two federal indictments have already been tossed, and the former president is attempting to do the same in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9
